name: ffuf (Fuzz Faster U Fool)

auth.notes: No auth for tool itself; pass cookies/headers for authenticated scanning

performance.notes: Extremely fast Go-based fuzzer; rate limiting recommended to avoid overloading targets

agent_readiness.notes: Fast web fuzzer for directory discovery, vhost enumeration, parameter fuzzing. ffuf -u https://target/FUZZ -w wordlist.txt. FUZZ keyword replaced by wordlist entries. JSON output: -of json -o results.json. Filters: -fc (filter status code), -fs (filter size), -fw (filter words), -fl (filter lines). Vhost: -H 'Host: FUZZ' -w vhosts.txt. Parameter fuzzing: /path?FUZZ=value. Rate limiting: -rate 100. Recursive: -recursion. Popular with SecLists wordlists. Faster than dirb/dirbuster.

gotchas: Only scan authorized targets — unauthorized scanning is illegal
High request rates can DoS target applications
Results require manual analysis for false positives
No built-in authentication flow (manual cookie/token passing)
best_when: API endpoint fuzzing for hidden routes
Parameter discovery and injection point finding
avoid_when: When Burp Suite Pro intruder provides better workflow integration
summary: ffuf is the fastest open-source web fuzzer. FUZZ keyword placement in URL/headers/body replaces with wordlist entries. JSON output for automation. Status/size/word/line filters to eliminate noise. Used by pentesters for directory discovery, vhost enum, parameter fuzzing. Pairs with SecLists. Only use on authorized targets.
